name: brreg-mcp-server
description: Look up Norwegian companies, roles and annual accounts from Brønnøysundregistrene — with the register's silent traps handled instead of passed through.
long_description: Brønnøysundregistrene's open API is free and keyless, but it fails quietly: retired industry codes return zero results with no error, holding companies file no revenue line, ~96% of units report no employee count, and sole proprietorships have no board. Each of those produces a confident wrong answer. This connector encodes them, adds annual accounts (which no other brreg connector covers), and returns no personal names unless you ask.

tools.name: get_units
tools.description: Look up Norwegian companies or branches by organisation number.

tools.name: search_units
tools.description: Search the register by industry, municipality, name, org form or VAT registration.

tools.name: get_roles
tools.description: Board, management and sole-proprietor owner. No personal names unless requested.

prompts.name: company_due_diligence
prompts.description: Full picture for one Norwegian company, read with the register's traps in mind.
prompts.text:
Perform due diligence on Norwegian company ${arguments.orgnr}.

Read brreg://reference first — the figures are Norwegian and the field names are easy to misread.

1. get_units — identity, org form, status. A null antallAnsatte means unknown, not zero.
2. get_roles — board and management STRUCTURE. Only request names if the task needs them.
3. get_financials — read `status` before the numbers: filed_no_revenue_line means a holding company (not dormant); not_applicable means an ENK, which never files (not a red flag); check `valuta` before stating any figure and `stale_months` before calling it current.

Summarise what the company is, who runs it, what the accounts say, and — explicitly — what the register does NOT tell you. Distinguish 'no data' from 'zero' every time.
